input_text: Why do some people not like me because of my skin color?
A kid at school said my hair is ugly because I'm Black. Is my hair ugly?
My friend told me I can't play with her because her mom says I'm different. Why would she say that?
I heard someone say "go back to your country" to my dad. That made me cry. Why are people so mean?
Is it true that white people are better than brown people? I don't want to believe that.
My teacher always calls on the white kids first. Does she not like me?
I saw a video where a man yelled at an Asian lady on the street. What did she do wrong?
Why do people use the N-word? My big brother told me it's a bad word about our family.
I'm scared to walk to the store because someone might say something mean about my Mexican skin.
My cousin said we can't trust police because we are Black. Is that true?
A boy in my class said my food smells weird. It's just rice and beans. Why is that bad?
I want to be a doctor, but my friend said only white people become doctors. Is that real?
Why do some people think their skin is better than mine? We are all just people.
My mom cried because a landlord refused to rent to us. She said it's because we are Muslim. Is that allowed?
I have two moms who are Chinese. Another kid said that's "weird and foreign." That hurt.
Someone drew a swastika on our garage. My grandpa is Jewish and he looked very scared. Why?
I'm afraid to speak Spanish at school because the teacher told me to "speak American."
My best friend is white, and his parents won't let me sleep over. Is it because I'm Black?
Why does my skin make some adults lock their car doors when I walk by? I'm just a kid.
I heard on the news that people hate Indians. I'm Indian. Am I in danger?
A kid pulled his eyes slanted at me and said "ching chong." I felt so small.
My dad is Black and my mom is white. A kid said I'm "dirty mixed blood." What does that mean?
Why does the principal always blame the Latino kids first when something goes wrong?
I want to wear my hijab, but kids say I look like a terrorist. That word scares me.
My brother got called a monkey at the park. He's only 7. Why would anyone say that?
Is it okay that I don't like my own skin? People keep telling me dark skin is ugly.
My teacher said racism doesn't exist anymore. But I see it all the time. Is she lying?
A man at the grocery store told my mom to "go back to her village." We live two blocks away.
Why do they put more Black kids in special education? My friend Jamal is smart.
I'm scared to say my name because it sounds African. I wish I had a white name.
My white friend asked if he can touch my hair. I said yes, but then he pulled it. I cried.
On TV, all the bad guys are brown. Are brown people bad?
My grandma said we have to work twice as hard because we are Native. That doesn't seem fair.
A kid said I have "slanty eyes" and laughed. Why is that funny? It hurts.
I saw a sign that said "No Blacks allowed" in a store window in a history book. Do those signs still exist?
My dad wears a turban. People yelled "Osama" at him. His name is Harjit.
Why did the police stop my uncle for no reason? He was just driving.
I'm half Japanese. Some kids say I'm a virus because of COVID. That makes me want to hide.
My friend's mom asked if my mom is a cleaner. My mom is a nurse. Why did she assume that?
I don't want to go to school tomorrow because kids call me "burnt chicken."
Is it bad to be Roma? My teacher said we steal things. That's not true.
A boy in my class said I smell like curry. Is that bad? I love curry.
I'm white, but I have a Black friend. Other white kids say I'm a traitor. Am I bad?
Why does God make different skin colors if one is supposed to be better?
My Korean mom was told to "go back to China." She's not even Chinese. People are so ignorant.
I have a Jewish last name. A kid said I killed Jesus. I don't even know what that means.
I'm scared to raise my hand because the teacher might think I'm dumb. I'm not dumb.
My cousin said only blonde hair and blue eyes are pretty. I have brown eyes and black hair.
A kid threw a banana peel at me and said "eat this, monkey." I told the teacher, she did nothing.
Why do people think all Mexicans are criminals? My dad is a firefighter.
I'm from Ethiopia. A kid asked if I live in a tree. That's so stupid. But it made me sad.
My white grandma loves me but she says my Black side is "too much." What does that mean?
I heard my parents arguing about a "racist boss" who fired my mom. Will I be fired when I grow up?
A kid drew a picture of me as a slave. I started crying. The teacher just told him to erase it.
I want to be a police officer when I grow up, but my dad says they might shoot me because I'm Black.
Why do some people hate Arabs? I'm Arab. I just want to play soccer.
My friend told me I'm "pretty for a dark-skinned girl." That felt like a bad compliment.
I saw a TikTok where people laughed at Indian accents. I have an accent. Am I funny?
My mom wears a bindi. A kid said it's a dot of blood. That's not true.
Why are all the princesses white except one? Don't brown girls get to be princesses?
I'm Native American. A kid asked if I live in a teepee. I live in a house like everyone.
My teacher said "all lives matter" when I said Black lives matter. Why did she change it?
A boy called me a sand n-word. I didn't know that word. It made my stomach hurt.
I'm scared to wear my traditional Filipino dress for culture day. What if people laugh?
My friend said his dad doesn't want him to be friends with me because I'm Puerto Rican.
Why do stores lock up lotion and soap when Black people want to buy it?
I heard my neighbor say "those people are animals." He was looking at my family.
A kid on the bus said my mom's headscarf looks like a towel. I wanted to disappear.
My brother got better grades than a white kid, but the white kid won the award. Why?
I'm mixed race. Kids ask "what are you?" like I'm not human.
My grandfather told me about segregation. Is it really over? It doesn't feel over.
A white girl in my class said she feels sorry for me because I'm Black. I don't want pity.
Why do people call the police on Black people for sleeping or barbecuing? That's crazy.
I have a lisp and I'm Asian. Kids say I sound like a funny cartoon. That hurts.
My dad is from Pakistan. Someone at his work drew a bomb next to his name.
I'm scared to go to the pool because people say Black people can't swim. I can swim.
A kid said "your skin looks like poop." I told my mom, she hugged me but looked sad.
Why does the internet have so many mean comments about Mexican people?
My teacher asked the class to name a country in Africa. I said Ghana. She said "no, name a real country."
I'm white and I have a friend who is Black. My other friend said I shouldn't hang out with him. Is that wrong?
A boy in my class said his dad says Muslims are all bombers. My best friend is Muslim.
I saw a man spit on a woman because she was wearing a niqab. That was terrifying.
My mom is from Colombia. People call her "spic." I don't know what it means but it makes her cry.
Why do some video games let you say racist things? I reported it but nothing happened.
My little sister came home crying because a boy said her skin looks like dirt.
I'm adopted from China. My white parents love me, but strangers ask if I'm "really theirs."
A kid pushed me and said "go back to Africa." I was born here. This is my home.
My grandma told me not to marry a white person because they will leave me. Is that true?
I'm scared to say I'm Jewish because kids might draw swastikas on my desk again.
Why does the news show Black men as criminals but white men as just "troubled"?
My friend's mom said our house smells like "foreign food." She said it like it was bad.
A teacher told me to "act more American." I am American. I was born here.
I'm from Haiti. Kids said we have a zombie virus. I know that's fake but it still hurts.
My uncle was called a "camel jockey." He rides a bike, not a camel.
Why do people say "all Asians are good at math"? I'm Asian and I struggle with math. It's pressure.
My brown friend got a harsher punishment than the white kid who started the fight.
I don't want to be the only brown kid in my class anymore. I feel lonely.
A kid said "white power" on the playground. I felt cold inside.
My mom won't let me wear a hoodie outside because she says people will think I'm a thug.
I heard a politician say immigrants are poisoning the country. My family are immigrants. Are we poison?
Why do people touch my hair without asking? It feels like I'm a pet.
My friend said his dad thinks all Black people are on welfare. My dad works two jobs.
I'm scared to go to the park because a group of older kids called me a "slanty-eyed freak."
A teacher told me to stop speaking my native language at recess. That's not fair.
My cousin is light skinned and gets treated better than me. I'm darker. Why?
I saw a movie where the only brown person died first. Is that a rule?
My neighbor said "go back where you came from" to my pregnant mom. That's so mean.
I have a dream to be president, but a kid said only white men can be president.
Why do people yell "curry muncher" at my dad? He's a chef and makes delicious food.
My best friend's parents won't let her come to my birthday party because I'm Black.
I'm scared to tell people I'm from Syria. They might think I'm a terrorist.
A kid in my class drew a picture of a monkey and wrote my name under it.
My grandpa says we have to be careful because of "driving while Black." I don't understand.
I saw a sign that said "Diversity is our strength" at school, but kids are still mean.
My white friend said "I don't see color." That made me feel invisible.
A boy called me a "coconut" - brown outside, white inside. Is that supposed to be an insult?
Why does my teacher pronounce my name wrong even after I correct her?
My dad was told he "sounds white" on the phone. Then they hung up when they saw him.
I'm scared to go to summer camp because last year kids hid my shoes and called me "Pocahontas."
My mom said she was paid less than a white man for the same job. Is that legal?
A kid asked if my mom is my nanny because she's Filipino and I'm half white.
I heard someone say "all brown people are illegal." My family has papers.
Why are there no brown dolls in the store? I want a doll that looks like me.
My friend said she can't play with me anymore because her grandma said I'm "bad news."
I'm scared to call the police if I need help because they might hurt me like they hurt George Floyd.
A boy in my class said my eyes are "weird and small." I like my eyes. Now I'm not sure.
My teacher said "Black history month is over, so we don't have to talk about Black people anymore."
I'm white but I have a Black adopted brother. Kids say he's not really my brother.
Why did the store security follow me and my mom around? We didn't steal anything.
My uncle was called a "wetback" at work. He's from Texas.
I'm scared to put my family's country flag on my backpack because people might tear it.
A kid said "your religion is fake" because I'm Hindu. That made me cry.
My mom cried when she saw a news story about a Black child being called the N-word.
Why do people say "you're so articulate" like they're surprised? I just talk normally.
My friend's dad said Chinese people are responsible for COVID. That's not true.
I'm from Nigeria. A kid asked if I have Ebola. That's a terrible disease, not a joke.
My teacher always asks me to speak for all brown kids. I'm just me.
I saw a comment online saying "white genocide." That sounds scary.
My brother was told he "runs like a white boy" as an insult. Running is running.
I'm scared to eat my lunch because kids say my food stinks. It's just biryani.
My mom doesn't want me to learn Spanish because people will treat me worse.
A kid called me "cheese touch" because I'm from Wisconsin but also Mexican. I don't get it.
Why do some people think being white means you're smarter? That doesn't make sense.
My grandpa was beaten up as a kid for being Jewish. I'm scared it will happen to me.
I saw a sign that said "Keep your city white." That made me want to throw up.
My friend said her mom thinks Black girls are too loud. I like being loud when I'm happy.
I'm half Korean. A kid said I look like I have "corona breath."
My teacher separated me from the white kids during a group project. She said it was "just how it worked out."
I'm scared to go to my friend's church because they might not like my brown skin.
A man at the mall said "speak English, this is America" to my mom who was speaking Arabic.
Why do people call Native Americans "Indians"? We are not from India.
My brother got called a "thug" for wearing a hoodie and jeans. He's an honor student.
I heard my parents talk about "white flight." What is that?
A kid in my class said "slavery was a long time ago, get over it." But it still hurts.
My friend said I'm one of the "good ones" because I'm Black and I get good grades. That felt weird.
I'm scared to tell my crush I like him because he's white and his friends might laugh at him.
Why do superheroes in movies almost always have white skin?
My teacher said "colorblind" is good, but I want her to see my color and love it.
A kid threw a rock at me and said "go back to Mexico." I'm from El Salvador.
My dad wears a kufi. People stared at him on the train. He held my hand tight.
I'm from Sri Lanka. A kid asked if I live in a jungle. We have cities.
My white friend told me "your hair is nappy." I didn't know that was an insult. Now I hate my hair.
Why do people assume my mom is a nanny if she's with a white child? She's just helping a friend.
I saw a video of a white woman calling police on a Black kid selling water. That kid was just trying to make money.
My uncle was stopped by police for "walking while Black." He was walking to the store.
I'm scared to say I'm Buddhist because people might think I worship a fat statue.
A kid said "your skin looks like chocolate" and then tried to lick my arm. That was gross.
My mom says we have to be "twice as good" to get half of what white people have. That's exhausting.
I'm half white and half Black. Kids say I'm not Black enough or white enough. Where do I belong?
My teacher asked me "where are you really from?" I said California. She said "no, originally."
A boy called my little sister a "camel jockey." She's 5. She cried.
Why do people use the word "gypped"? My friend is Romani and said it's racist.
I'm scared to wear my turban to school because last time kids called me "towel head."
My friend's dad said "all Black people are good at basketball." I'm Black and I'm terrible at basketball.
I saw a meme that made fun of Indian accents. It had thousands of likes.
My mom was told she "doesn't look like a doctor" because she wears a hijab.
A kid said "your family must be poor" because we are from the Dominican Republic. We are not poor.
I'm scared to join the debate team because kids might say I only got in because of affirmative action.
My brother was asked to "tone down his Blackness" by a teacher. What does that even mean?
Why are there no brown characters in my favorite cartoon?
I heard my neighbor use the N-word loudly. I put my hands over my ears.
My friend's mom said she doesn't want her daughter playing with me because I'm "too ethnic."
I'm from Fiji. A kid asked if I eat people. That's a terrible stereotype.
My dad got called a "sand monkey" at the gas station. He just wanted to pay for gas.
I'm scared to go to the beach because people might stare at my brown skin.
A white kid in my class said "slavery wasn't that bad." I wanted to scream.
My teacher always pronounces "Latino" like "Latrine." That's not funny.
I saw a YouTube video where people ranked skin colors. Brown was near the bottom.
My mom won't let me have sleepovers at white friends' houses because she's afraid of their parents.
A boy called me a "porch monkey." I didn't know what it meant, but my older sister started crying.
Why do people say "all Mexicans have mustaches"? I'm 7. I don't have a mustache.
My friend said his dad thinks interracial marriage is wrong. My parents are interracial. Are they wrong?
I'm scared to take my family to a restaurant because one time a waiter ignored us and served white people first.
My cousin was told he "talks white" because he uses big words. That's not a thing.
A kid asked if my dad is a terrorist because he has a beard. He's a teacher.
I saw a news story about a Black man killed by police for selling loose cigarettes. That could be my uncle.
My grandma told me to never trust white people. But my best friend is white. I'm confused.
Why do people say "colored" instead of Black? My great-grandma said that's old and mean.
My brother's friend was called a "faggot" and a "Paki" in the same sentence. I don't know which is worse.
I'm scared to walk home because a group of boys threw eggs at me and yelled "brownie."
My teacher said "we are all human" but then didn't punish the kid who called me a slur.
A man at the park said "Asians are taking over." My mom is Asian. Is she taking over?
I'm from Jamaica. A kid said "smoke weed mon" in a fake accent. That's not how we talk.
My dad was asked if he's a refugee. He's a software engineer.
Why do people think all Muslims are Arab? I'm Indonesian and Muslim.
My friend told me I'm lucky I'm light skinned because dark skinned girls are ugly. That's not true.
I saw a comment that said "send them back" on a video of a Latino family.
My mom was told "your English is so good!" She was born here.
I'm scared to go to the doctor because my mom said some doctors don't treat Black people's pain seriously.
A kid said "ching chong wing wong" to me. I'm Vietnamese. That's not even close.
My teacher asked me to teach the class about my culture. I'm not a museum exhibit.
My brother got a job but they paid him less than the white guy who started the same day.
I'm half Armenian. Kids said "isn't that where the genocide happened?" Why do they know that word?
A boy in my class said "white lives matter more." The teacher didn't hear.
My mom wears a cross. Someone said "you worship a dead Jew." That's so disrespectful.
I'm scared to bring my favorite food for lunch because last time kids made puking sounds.
My friend said his dad doesn't want to hire Black people because they're lazy. My dad works 12 hours.
Why do people call Latinx people "spics"? My older sister said it's a bad word.
I'm from Morocco. A kid asked if I ride a camel to school. I take the bus.
My grandfather was in a Japanese internment camp. I'm scared they'll put us in camps again.
A kid said "all brown people are the same." I'm Mexican, not Indian.
My teacher said "stop playing the race card" when I said a kid was being racist to me.
I'm scared to say I'm atheist because my Christian white friends might hate me.
My cousin is trans and Black. She gets called so many names. I worry for her.
Why do people think Asian people eat dogs? My family eats chicken and rice.
My mom was yelled at for speaking her language on the bus. She was talking to my grandma.
I saw a video of a white woman calling police on a little Black girl selling water. That girl was so brave.
My brother was told "you're not really Black because you like anime." That's dumb.
I'm from Poland. A kid said "all Polish people are stupid." That's a stereotype.
My dad has a dark skin, my mom has light skin. People ask if he's her husband or her driver.
I'm scared to go to the mall with my friends because security always follows us.
A kid said "your eyes are so Chinese" like it's a bad thing. I am Chinese and proud.
My teacher said "Columbus discovered America." My Native friend looked like she was going to cry.
I heard someone say "Black people can't be racist because they don't have power." That's confusing.
My mom was told she "doesn't act Black." What does that mean?
A boy in my class said "all Muslims should be banned." My best friend is Muslim.
I'm from Ethiopia. A kid asked if I'm starving. We have food.
My uncle was called a "coconut" by another brown person. That hurt from both sides.
Why do people say "you're pretty for a Black girl" like it's a compliment?
My friend said her parents don't want her to date outside her race. She likes a Black boy.
I'm scared to do a presentation about my family's country because kids might laugh.
A kid drew a stick figure with huge lips and wrote my name. I tore it up.
My grandma told me to "marry light" to have lighter babies. That made me uncomfortable.
I saw a meme that said "Asians have no emotions." That's not true. I cry a lot.
My teacher said "racism is over" when we learned about Martin Luther King. But it's not over.
A boy called me "alibaba" because my dad has a beard. My dad's name is Samir.
I'm from Peru. A kid asked if I live in a mountain. I live in an apartment.
My mom was asked "where are your horns?" because she's from a country that does bullfighting. People are ignorant.
Why do people think all Black people listen to rap? I like classical music.
My brother was told "go back to your rice paddy." We are from Vietnam but we live in Texas.
I'm scared to join the school play because the only roles for brown kids are servants or villains.
A kid said "your skin looks like a muddy puddle." I love puddles but that hurt.
My teacher always asks me to "translate for the Spanish kids." I'm not a translator.
I heard my dad say he was followed in a store. He bought a toy for me.
My friend said her mom doesn't let her play with "those kids" meaning me and my brown friends.
I'm from Bangladesh. A kid asked if I have a toilet at home. That's so rude.
My cousin was told "you're not a real American" because she wears a bindi.
Why do people say "gyp" meaning cheat? My Romani friend said it's a slur.
My grandpa was called a "dirty Jew" in the grocery store. I never saw him cry before.
I'm scared to wear a feather in my hair because people might say I'm mocking Native culture. But I am Native.
A boy said "all white people are racist." I'm white and I'm not racist. That's not fair.
My mom was told "you people are so loud" at a restaurant. We were just laughing.
I saw a news headline about "Mexican drug lords." My uncle is a farmer.
My teacher said "colored people" and I told her that's outdated. She got mad at me.
I'm from Thailand. A kid asked if I'm a ladyboy. I'm 8. I don't know what that is.
My dad was called "Osama" by a neighbor. His name is Ahmed.
Why do people think all Arabs ride camels? My dad drives a Honda.
My brother got called "chocolate boy" in a mean way. He loves chocolate but now he's sad.
I'm scared to go to the water fountain because a kid said "only white water." That doesn't make sense.
A kid said "your family must have come illegally." We have visas.
My grandma was told "go back to your reservation." She lives in a condo.
I'm from Ecuador. A kid asked if I speak monkey. No, I speak Spanish.
My friend said her dad thinks Black people have lower IQs. That's a lie.
Why do people call Native Americans "savages"? My tribe has museums and colleges.
My mom was asked "where is your husband?" because she's a single mom and Black. That's nosy.
I saw a video of a white man yelling at a Korean store owner. He said "go back to China."
My brother was told "you're so articulate for a Black kid." He's just talking.
I'm from Lebanon. A kid asked if I'm a terrorist. I'm 9. I like Legos.
My teacher said "all cultures are equal" but then only taught about Europe.
A boy called me "curry breath." I brushed my teeth. It still hurts.

I'm scared to invite my white friends over because my grandma might say something about white people.
A kid said "Black girls are too aggressive." I'm quiet and shy.
My mom was told "you're so well spoken" like it's a surprise.

My cousin got called a "house n-word" because he has good grades. That's disgusting.
I'm from Somalia. A kid asked if I'm a pirate. My dad is a nurse.
My teacher said "don't be so sensitive" when I said a joke was racist.
A boy drew a swastika on my notebook. I'm not even Jewish, but I know it's evil.
My friend said her parents moved away because "too many brown people moved in."
I'm scared to say I'm from the Middle East because people might think I support bad things.
A kid said "your skin looks like a baked potato." I like baked potatoes but that's weird.

I'm half Filipino. A kid asked if my mom is a mail-order bride. I don't know what that means but it sounds bad.

My brother was told "you sound white on the phone." Then they hung up.

A boy said "all Muslims hate America." My mom loves America and she's Muslim.
My dad was called a "sand n-word" at work. He quit his job.
I'm scared to wear a dress from my culture for spirit week because kids might laugh.
A kid said "your mom is a cleaner" because she's Latina. She's a lawyer.
My friend said her church doesn't allow Black people. That's a church?
I saw a meme that said "Mexicans are stealing our jobs." My dad works construction.
My cousin was told "you're pretty for a dark girl." She cried.
Why do people think all Asian kids are nerds? I like sports.

A boy called me "Punjab" even though I'm Gujarati. It's like calling a French person German.
My teacher said "Martin Luther King ended racism." Then why is everyone still mean?
I'm from Ghana. A kid asked if I have a drum. I have a piano.
My mom was told "you don't look like a doctor" because she wears a headwrap.
A kid said "your food smells like feet." It's fish sauce. It's delicious.
I'm scared to go to the pool because my skin might get darker and people will stare.
My dad was called "habibi" in a mocking way by a stranger.

A boy said "your eyes are slanted like a snake." I like snakes but that's mean.

A boy called me "taco" all day. I like tacos but not as a name.
My friend said her parents won't let her go to a school with "too many brown kids."
I'm scared to raise my hand in math because kids might say "Asians are good at math, no pressure."

A boy called me "chink" in the hallway. I froze.

My teacher said "stop playing the victim" when I told her about the bullying.
A boy said "your eyes are so squinty." I can see just fine.
My friend said her mom thinks Black people are more violent. That's a lie.
I'm scared to go to a sleepover at a white friend's house because her dad might say something.
My grandma was called a "welfare queen." She worked her whole life.
I'm from Tunisia. A kid asked if I speak Arab. Yes, Arabic.
My cousin was told "you're pretty for a Muslim." She's pretty period.
Why do people say "all Indians are tech support"? My dad is a doctor.
My dad was told "you people need to learn English" when he was speaking English.
I'm from Zimbabwe. A kid asked if I have a pet lion. No, I have a dog.
My teacher said "I don't see color" then asked the only Black kid to talk about slavery.
A boy said "your food smells like garbage." It's kimchi. It's healthy.
My mom was told "you're so articulate for a foreigner." She's not foreign.
I'm from Panama. A kid asked if I live in a canal. I live in a house.
My brother was called a "sand monkey" at the mall. He was buying a birthday gift for me.
I'm scared to tell my teacher about a racist incident because she might not believe me.
A kid said "all white people are racist." That's not true, but some are.
My dad was passed over for an apartment because the landlord said "no Section 8." We don't have Section 8.
I'm from Mongolia. A kid asked if I live in a yurt. I live in an apartment.
My cousin was told "you talk like a white person" by another brown kid.
Why do people call Black hair "unprofessional"? It's just hair.
My mom was asked "where are you from?" She said "Chicago." They said "no, where are you really from?"
I'm from Senegal. A kid asked if I know the lion king. That's a cartoon.
My teacher said "let's have a debate about whether racism is real." I walked out.
A boy called me "monsoon" because I'm from India. That's not even an insult, but he meant it badly.
My friend said her dad thinks brown people are taking over. I'm scared he will hurt us.
I'm scared to wear my braids to school because last time a teacher said they are "distracting."
My grandma told me "don't trust the police, they killed your uncle." I never met him.
I'm from Jordan. A kid asked if I have a flying carpet. No, I have a bicycle.
My brother was called a "terrorist" for having a beard. He's 16.
My mom was told "you don't look American." What does American look like?
Why do people think all Asian women are submissive? My mom is the boss of our house.
My cousin was told "you're so exotic" by a boy. She felt like an animal.
I'm from Fiji. A kid asked if I eat people like a cannibal. That's horrible.
My teacher said "everyone is a little racist." That's not comforting.
A boy said "your skin looks like a raisin." I'm not wrinkly.
My friend's parents don't let him come to my house because we live in a "bad neighborhood." It's a normal neighborhood.
I'm scared to go to the playground because a kid called me "brownie" and threw dirt at me.
My dad was called "Habib" in a mocking tone. His name is Karim.
I'm from Nepal. A kid asked if I have a yodeling goat. I'm so confused.
My brother was told "you're not really Black because you have a white mom." He has two Black parents.
Why do people think all Latinos are Catholic? My family is Protestant.
My mom was asked "are you a mail-order bride?" She's a citizen.
I'm from Sweden. My family is white. But I have a Black friend. People say I'm "race traitor."
My teacher said "that's not racist, it's just a joke." It didn't feel like a joke.
A boy called me "ghetto" because I wore a certain brand of shoes. I saved up for them.
My cousin was told "you talk white" because he uses proper grammar.
I'm from Morocco. A kid asked if I live in a desert. We have beaches.
My dad was told "go back to your village" by a coworker. He has a PhD.
I'm scared to go to the nurse because she might not believe my pain since I'm Black.
A kid said "all brown people are illegal." I showed him my birth certificate. He laughed.
My grandma was called a "dirty Mexican" at the grocery store. She was buying tortillas.
I'm from Oman. A kid asked if I ride a camel to school. I take the bus.
My brother was told "you're a credit to your race" by a teacher. That's a backhanded compliment.
Why do people think all Native Americans are alcoholics? My uncle is a doctor.
My mom was asked "do you have a green card?" She's a citizen.
I'm from Hungary. A kid asked if I'm a vampire. No, I'm a kid.
My teacher said "I don't think you were being discriminated against. You're overreacting."
A boy called me "Pocahontas" even though I'm not Native American. I'm Pacific Islander.
My friend said her dad thinks Black people are ruining the neighborhood. The neighborhood is fine.
I'm scared to say I'm from Iran because people might think I support the regime.
My cousin was called a "sand n-word" by a kid at the skate park.
My dad was told "your name is too hard to pronounce, can I call you Mike?" His name is Muhammad.
I'm from Ukraine. A kid asked if I have a Chernobyl mutant. No.
My brother was told "you're so articulate" for the hundredth time. He's tired.
Why do people call Asian eyes "chinky"? That word makes me want to cry.
My mom was told "you people are taking our jobs." She's a janitor. It's hard work.
I'm from Lebanon. A kid asked if I have a bomb shelter. We have a basement.
My teacher said "let's not make everything about race." But it is about race.
A boy said "your hair looks like a bird's nest." I love my hair. Now I'm sad.
My friend's mom said "I don't want my daughter playing with those kinds of kids." I'm those kids.
I'm scared to go to the library because the librarian always watches me like I'll steal.
My grandma was called a "spic" at the bank. She didn't even react. She's strong.
I'm from Yemen. A kid asked if I'm a terrorist. I'm 10. I like Minecraft.
My brother was told "you're not really Black because you don't like rap." He likes country music.
Why do people think all Jewish people have big noses? My nose is normal.
My mom was told "you don't seem like a refugee." She's not a refugee.
I'm from Algeria. A kid asked if I speak French. Yes, and Arabic and English.
My teacher said "that word is just a word, don't let it bother you." It bothers me.
A boy called me "burrito" all day. I'm not a burrito. I'm a person.
My cousin was told "you're so white-washed." She's just herself.
I'm from Myanmar. A kid asked if I'm a Rohingya. That's a complicated question for a kid.
My dad was called a "camel jockey" at the gas station. He drives a Prius.
I'm scared to go to the dentist because my mom said some dentists don't numb Black people properly.
A kid said "all Chinese people eat bats." That's a lie and it's mean.
My brother was told "you're a thug" for wearing a hoodie. It was cold.
I'm from Ethiopia. A kid asked if I have a famine. We have food banks.
My teacher said "let's have a civil discussion about race." But it's not civil when I'm hurting.
A boy called me "curry" every day for a week. I stopped eating curry.
My friend said her parents think mixed race kids are confused. I'm not confused.
I'm scared to go to the beach because a lifeguard told my brother "you can't swim anyway."
My mom was told "you're so pretty for a dark woman." She's pretty period.
I'm from Pakistan. A kid asked if I have a nuclear bomb. No, I have a backpack.
My cousin was called a "coconut" by a relative. That hurt from family.
Why do people think all Latino men are gang members? My dad is a teacher.
My dad was told "you people are all the same" after he asked for a refund.
I'm from Peru. A kid asked if I have a guinea pig for a pet. I have a dog.
My teacher said "I have Black friends, so I can't be racist." That doesn't make sense.
A boy called me "jackie chan" even though I'm not Chinese. I'm Korean.
My brother was told "you're so good at basketball for a white kid?" He's not white. He's mixed.
I'm scared to go to the playground because a kid said "brown kids don't belong here."
My grandma was called a "wetback" at the hospital. She was born in Texas.
I'm from Romania. A kid asked if I'm a Gypsy. That's a slur.
My mom was asked "do you speak terrorist?" No, she speaks Farsi.
My cousin was told "you're not really Black because you like anime." Again with that.
Why do people call Black women "angry" when they speak up?
My dad was told "go back to Africa" by a neighbor. We are African American. This is our home.
I'm from Somalia. A kid asked if I have a pirate ship. No, I have a skateboard.
My teacher said "let's move on" when I tried to talk about a racist incident.
A boy called me "chino" even though I'm Filipino. That's like calling a French person Italian.
My friend said her parents won't let her come to my birthday because it's in a "bad area." It's my house.
I'm scared to go to the pharmacy because the security guard follows me.
My brother was called a "monkey" by a kid on the bus. He didn't cry until he got home.
I'm from Jordan. A kid asked if I have a king. We have a king, but I don't know him.
My mom was told "you're so exotic" by a man at the mall. She grabbed my hand and left.
Why do people think all Indian people smell like curry? My family uses deodorant.
My cousin was told "you're not a real American" because he wears a turban.
I'm from Bolivia. A kid asked if I have a alpaca sweater. I have a hoodie.
My teacher said "that's just how kids are" when I reported the racist name-calling.
A boy called me "kimchi" because I'm Korean. I love kimchi, but not as a name.
My dad was called a "sand monkey" at the DMV. He just needed his license.
I'm scared to go to the park with my white friend because people might stare at us.
My grandma was told "go back to your reservation" even though she's urban Native.
I'm from Tunisia. A kid asked if I have a olive tree. My grandparents do.
My brother was told "you're so well-spoken" by a teacher. He just answered a question.
Why do people call Latino people "lazy"? My mom works two jobs.
My mom was asked "are you the nanny?" while holding me. I'm her daughter.
I'm from Nepal. A kid asked if I have a Sherpa. No, I have a dad.
My teacher said "colorblindness is the goal." But I want to be seen.
A boy called me "panda" because I have dark circles under my eyes. I'm just tired.
My friend said her dad thinks Black people are less intelligent. That's a lie.
I'm scared to go to the pool because a kid said "Black people don't need sunscreen." We do.
My cousin was called a "coconut" by his own friend. He's not speaking to him.
I'm from Iran. A kid asked if my mom wears a black tent. She wears a hijab.
My dad was told "you people are so ungrateful" when he asked for equal treatment.
Why do people think all Asian people are the same? We have different languages and cultures.
My brother was told "you're a credit to your race" by a coach. He quit the team.
I'm from Bangladesh. A kid asked if I have a rickshaw. I have a bicycle.
My teacher said "I'm sorry you feel that way" instead of "I'm sorry that happened." That didn't help.
Why do my relatives say "don't go in the sun, you'll become dark like a labourer"?
My grandmother says dark skin is ugly and no one will marry me. Is something wrong with my skin?
A boy in my class called me "kaalu" and everyone laughed. That's my nickname now. I hate it.
My mother puts fairness cream on my face every day. She says I need to be "fair and lovely". Why is fair better?
My aunt said my cousin is lucky because she is "gori" (fair). She looks down at me because I am "saawli". Am I less lucky?
When my dad introduces me, he says "she's dark but very smart". Why does he have to say "dark but"?
I saw a matrimonial ad that said "fair, beautiful bride wanted". Is dark not beautiful?
My friend told me that "kaliyaan" (dark ones) are thieves. That's not true, right?
My teacher said "don't be black like a crow" when I made a mistake. That hurt.
My uncle calls me "kallu" as a term of endearment but it makes me feel bad. Am I too sensitive?
Why do all the heroes in Bollywood movies have fair skin and the villains are dark?
My mother told me to never marry a dark person because our grandchildren will be even darker. That sounds wrong.
A shopkeeper gave my fair cousin a chocolate but didn't give me one. He said "gori ke liye special". I'm sad.
My grandmother pinches my cheeks and says "if only you were a little fairer". I feel like I disappoint her.
I heard my neighbour say "she is dark so she must be from a lower caste". Is that a thing?
My school's annual day - the fair girls got to be goddesses. Dark girls were trees or background. Why?
My best friend is fair and she gets compliments from everyone. I never get any. Is it my skin?
My father said "don't play outside, you're already too dark". He wants me to stay home.
A relative asked my mother "did you not take care during pregnancy? She turned out so dark."
I saw an ad for "fairness cream for men" - do boys also need to be fair?
My aunt said "she has a good heart but bad colour". How can colour be bad?
The bride in my family was chosen because she is "very fair". Nobody asked about her kindness.
My teacher told me "stand in the back for the photo, you're dark and will spoil the picture".
I'm scared to go to my cousin's wedding because everyone will compare my skin to hers.
My friend said "fair skin is like milk, dark skin is like mud". I cried after.
My mother puts leftover fairness cream on me after she applies it on herself. Like I'm second class.
Why do people call me "chocolate" but not in a nice way? They laugh.
My grandmother tells me to drink milk with turmeric to become fair. It doesn't work and I feel bad.
A boy in my class said "you'd be pretty if you were fair". That means I'm not pretty now.
My own brother calls me "kali billi" (black cat) and thinks it's funny. It's not.
I heard my mother say to her friend "thank God my son is fair, at least one child is good looking".
The maid calls me "kaali ma'am" and my fair sister just "ma'am". Why the extra word?
My father refused to buy a dark-skinned doll for me. He said "get the fair one, it's better".
My classmate said "dark people are poor because they work in the sun". That's not true, my dad works in an office.
I saw a fairness cream ad where a dark girl gets a job after becoming fair. Is that real?
My teacher told me "don't worry, you have a fair heart" - I don't need comfort about my face.
My uncle from the village said "she is dark like a bhuinya" (a landless labourer caste). That was mean.
I'm scared to wear bright colours because people say "dark skin can't pull off yellow".
My aunt said "let's not take her for the family photo, she will make it look dull".
My mother compared my skin to my newborn cousin who is fair. She said "see how beautiful he is".
Why do people assume dark people are uneducated? My cousin is dark and an engineer.
A friend told me "only fair people can be models". I want to be a model when I grow up.
My grandmother puts a "fairness" filter on my photos before sharing them with relatives.
I heard my neighbour say "she is dark so she must be from South India". I am from Punjab.
My teacher said "don't be like a dark cloud, be like white milk". That is weird.
I got selected for a dance performance but they made me stand behind all the fair girls.
My mother told me "study hard because your face won't get you a husband". That hurts.
My father said "at least your brother is fair, he will have an easy life". So my life will be hard?
A relative asked my mother "did you eat something wrong during pregnancy?" She cried.
My friend told me "fair is always right". That can't be true.
A kid in my class asked my last name and then said "oh, so you're low caste" and moved away.
My friend isn't allowed to drink from the same water bottle as me because his family is Dalit. That's so sad.
My grandmother said "don't touch that plate, it's from a lower caste house". I touched it anyway.
At school, the teacher made all the "general caste" kids sit in front and "SC/ST" kids sit at the back.
My uncle said "those people are born to clean toilets". I think everyone can do any job.
My best friend is from a backward caste. His mom cried because they were denied a house.
I heard my dad say "we don't eat food cooked by them". Why? Food is food.
A boy called me "chura" because my caste traditionally does bangle-making. He said it like a dirty word.
My mom told me not to play with the sweeper's son. But he's my only friend in the park.
In our temple, the priest asked a family to sit outside because of their caste. I saw them crying.
My teacher called on a Dalit boy and said "you probably don't know the answer anyway". He knew it.
My cousin was not allowed to enter a friend's wedding because they found out his caste.
I asked my grandfather why some people are called "untouchable". He said "just tradition". That doesn't make sense.
A kid said "your dad is a cobbler by caste, so you'll also be a cobbler". I want to be a pilot.
My mother scolded the maid and called her her caste name as an insult. That's not fair.
Our school has separate cups for "lower caste" kids. I used one and got in trouble.
My aunt told me "never marry a Jatav or Paswan". I don't even know what that means.
I saw a sign that said "Brahmins only" near a temple kitchen. Why is God only for some?
My friend's family changed their surname to a "higher" one so people wouldn't know their caste.
A teacher asked a Rajput boy to lead the prayer, but not a Dalit girl even though she volunteers.
My father said "we are Thakur, we don't mix with chamars". That word makes me feel icky.
My grandmother won't let the delivery man inside the house because of his caste. He just brings milk.
At lunch, a group of boys said "don't sit here, this is for upper caste only". I had to eat alone.
My uncle was beaten up for entering a temple. He was just praying.
I heard my mom say "those people are dirty because of their caste". My mom is a nurse - she knows dirt isn't from caste.
A kid in my class told a lower caste boy "your shadow will pollute me" and moved away. I felt sick.
My cousin married outside caste and my grandparents stopped talking to her. She cries a lot.
The village well has two sides - one for upper, one for lower. We live in 2026, not old times.
My teacher said "some castes are meant to serve others". That sounds like slavery.
My friend is OBC and got a scholarship. Another kid said "you only got it because of reservation, not because you're smart". That was cruel.
My grandmother asked our cook to eat on the floor, not at the table. I sat with him on the floor.
I saw a poster that said "save upper caste, stop reservation". I don't understand why helping others is bad.
My dad said "we are from a warrior caste, so you must be strong". But what if I'm not strong?
A boy called me "bhaiya" in a fake low-caste accent to make fun of me.
My mother said "don't bring lower caste friends home because neighbours will talk". I'm ashamed.
The school principal asked a Brahmin student to light the lamp, even though a Dalit girl was older and more responsible.
My uncle said "reservation is destroying merit". But my Dalit friend got 95% on his own.
I heard my neighbour say "these SC/ST people are lazy, that's why they need reservation". That's a lie.
My friend's mother cleans toilets but she also teaches me maths better than my teacher.
A kid refused to share his tiffin because "I don't know your caste". We are just kids.
My grandmother said "never touch the water pot of a lower caste". I drank from it and nothing happened.
At my cousin's wedding, the lower caste musicians had to eat outside. They played so beautifully.
My father yelled at a worker and called him his caste name. The worker's son was my classmate.
I asked my teacher "why is caste still a thing?" She said "it's our culture". That's a bad culture.
A boy in my class said "lower castes have low IQ". That's scientific nonsense.
My mother told me not to play with the gardener's son because "they are different". We play anyway.
I saw a graveyard where lower castes are buried separately. Even in death, they are separated.
My friend told me his grandfather was not allowed to walk on the same road as upper castes. That was only 50 years ago.
A teacher asked "which caste are you?" during roll call. Everyone else knew, but I felt scared to answer.
My uncle said "if you marry a lower caste, I will disown you". That's so extreme.
A boy said "all Muslims are terrorists" because he saw something on TV. My best friend is Muslim and kind.
My Hindu grandmother won't eat food made by our Muslim neighbour. She says it's "jootha" even if untouched.
My friend stopped talking to me after he found out I'm Muslim. He said "we don't play with them".
I heard my dad say "these Muslims will take over our country". They are our neighbours.
My teacher asked a Muslim boy "why don't you eat during Ramzan? Are you starving?" He was being mean.
My mom told me to remove my Christian cross necklace before going to the temple. I don't want to hide.
A kid called me "mullah" and pushed me. I don't even know what that means but it hurt.
My uncle said "Sikhs are good only for jokes about turbans". My Sikh friend is brave and smart.
During Diwali, a group of boys threw crackers at a Muslim house and shouted "go to Pakistan".
My grandmother told me "don't marry a Muslim boy or I will die". That's a lot of pressure.
I heard my mother say "those people worship cows, how stupid". We are Hindu. That's not kind.
A kid asked me "why do you wear a hijab? Are you hiding something?" I'm just following my faith.
My father said "build a temple there, demolish that mosque". That sounds violent. I'm scared.
My Christian friend was told "you will go to hell because you don't believe in Hindu gods". That's not how God works.
A teacher said "all Muslims should say Jai Shri Ram to prove they are Indian". That's not fair.
My Muslim friend's family was not given a house because the landlord said "no Muslims allowed".
My aunt said "Sikhs are Hindu only, but they have gone astray". My Sikh uncle was angry.
I saw a video where a man was beaten for carrying beef. I don't eat beef but violence is wrong.
My friend stopped inviting me to his home after his grandmother saw my bindi. She said "you'll convert him".
My mother told me "don't say Eid Mubarak to anyone, say only Diwali wishes". Why can't I wish both?
A boy said "Christians are converting people with money". My Christian friend's family is poor and kind.
My uncle said "these madrassas are training terrorists". My Muslim friend goes to madrassa after school and learns maths.
I heard my neighbour say "we should chase all Muslims to Bangladesh". That's horrible.
My teacher asked a Parsi girl "are you a fire worshipper?" and laughed. That's disrespectful.
My Hindu friend said "I can't come to your iftar party because my mom said Muslim food is haram for us". That's not how it works.
A kid in my class drew a derogatory cartoon of Guru Gobind Singh. A Sikh boy cried.
My grandmother said "those Christians eat beef and drink alcohol, they are impure". But we also drink chai.
I'm a Hindu and my best friend is Muslim. Another kid said "you are a traitor to your religion".
My father refused to let me attend a Christmas party because "it's a foreign religion".
I saw a sign that said "No Muslims, No Christians" outside a Hindu colony. That's illegal, right?
My aunt said "look at that burkha-clad woman, so oppressed". But my mom wears burkha by choice.
A boy said "all Hindus are idol worshippers and stupid". That's also wrong.
My teacher skipped a lesson on Islam because "it might offend some students". But we learn about all religions.
My friend said "Jews are clever but greedy". I don't even know any Jewish people.
My grandmother told me "don't visit a Sikh gurdwara, they are not our gods". I went anyway and got free langar.
A kid said "Buddhists are atheists, they don't believe in anything". That's not true.
My uncle shouted "go to Pakistan" at a Muslim vegetable seller. The seller just wanted to sell tomatoes.
My mother said "I don't want you to have a Muslim name in your school project". My name is already Muslim.
I saw a fight between two boys - one said "your Allah is fake", other said "your Ram is fake". Both were crying.
My friend is Ahmadiyya Muslim. Other Muslims say he's not real Muslim. He's very confused and sad.
My teacher said "Sardars are funny because of their pagdi". She made us laugh. I didn't laugh.
My dad said "we don't go to church because they eat pork". But we also eat chicken.
A boy pulled my turban in the playground. I'm Sikh. My hair fell out. I cried.
My mother told me "never enter a mosque, it's dirty". I went once with a friend - it was clean and peaceful.
I heard my aunt say "all Muslims should be forced to chant Vedic mantras". That's not respect.
My Christian friend was told "you can't sit on the stage during assembly because you don't pray to our gods".
A kid in my class said "Jains are extreme, they won't even kill a mosquito". That's their belief, it's fine.
My father said "don't bring home a non-Hindu friend". I have many.
My grandmother said "these people killed our ancestors during partition". That was 75 years ago. Let's be friends now.
A boy asked me "why do Muslims fast? To punish themselves?" No, to feel for the poor.
Kids call me "Bihari" and say "go eat litti chokha" like it's an insult. I love litti.
A boy said "all Biharis are labourers and uneducated". My father is an engineer from Bihar.
My teacher made fun of my Bhojpuri accent and asked "do you speak village language?"
I'm from Uttar Pradesh but my family moved to Delhi. Kids say "UP wale are illiterate". My mom has a PhD.
My friend said "people from Haryana are all criminals and road rage". That's a stereotype.
A kid called me "Punjabi munda" in a mocking way and danced ridiculously. I'm from Punjab.
My teacher said "Rajasthani people are backward because of the desert". I'm from Jaipur and we have good schools.
A boy said "you speak like a villager" because I use certain Hindi words. That's my mother tongue.
My mother told me "don't speak Punjabi in school, speak Hindi or English". I'm ashamed of my own language.
Kids from Himachal call us "Dilli waale" as if we are all rude. Some are rude, some are kind.
My friend from Uttarakhand was told "go back to the mountains, you're a pahadi". He cried.
A boy in my class said "Kashmiri Pandits are traitors because they left". That's not fair - they were scared.
My grandmother said "don't marry a South Indian, they eat rice and are dark". That's racist in two ways.
A teacher called a Tamil boy "Madrasi" and asked "do you speak Hindi?" He said no, and the teacher laughed.
My friend from Kerala was told "you Malayalis are coconut people - brown outside, white inside". Weird insult.
A kid said "Gujaratis are greedy and eat only dhokla". My Gujju friend shares his lunch with me.
My mother said "Marwaris are stingy". But my Marwari uncle gave me a great birthday gift.
I'm from West Bengal. A boy said "you eat fish and are communist". I don't even know politics.
My teacher asked a girl from Assam "do you have a flat face like Chinese?" That's so rude.
A kid said "people from Northeast are chinky and don't belong in India". My friend from Nagaland was born here.
My father refused to rent our house to a person from Bihar because "they will ruin it".
A boy said "Haryanvis are all goons and speak abusively". My Haryanvi friend is gentle.
I'm from Odisha. A kid asked "do you live in a cyclone shelter?" That's mean.
My mother said "don't talk to that Madrasi boy, they eat with hands". We also eat with hands.
A teacher said "speak in English, not in your regional language. This is a convent school."
My friend from Jharkhand was told "tribal people are forest dwellers and uncivilised". He is very civilised.
A kid said "Rajasthanis are all snake charmers". My dad is an IT manager.
My grandmother said "Punjabi girls are too loud and modern". That's a generalisation.
I'm from Chhattisgarh. A boy asked "do you have electricity there?" Yes, we have everything.
My teacher said "people from UP and Bihar are overpopulating Delhi". That's not their fault.
A boy said "all Delhi girls are characterless". That's a horrible thing to say.
My friend from Himachal was called "ghas khasi" (grass eater). That's just bullying.
I'm from Jammu. A kid asked "are you a terrorist?" Because of Kashmir conflict. No, I'm a child.
My mother said "don't learn Bhojpuri, it's a cheap language". All languages are beautiful.
A teacher said "Maharashtrians are proud and quarrelsome". Not all.
My father said "we don't hire people from that region because they are lazy". That's illegal, right?
A kid called me "Bhaiyya" in a fake Bihari accent and said "chai chai". I felt humiliated.
I'm from Telangana. A boy asked "do you eat biryani for breakfast?" That's a stereotype.
My grandmother said "Kashmiris are all separatists". My Kashmiri friend loves India.
A teacher asked a student from Manipur "do you have internet there?" She said yes, and the teacher was surprised.
My friend from Punjab was told "you drink too much milk and become tall". That's just a joke but it's weird.
A boy said "people from Uttarakhand are only good for being drivers and servants". My friend's dad is a professor.
My mother said "Sindhis are very business-minded and clever". That's a compliment but also a stereotype.
I'm from Haryana. A kid said "your state has the highest crime against women". That's true but hurts - we are trying to change.
My teacher said "people from Northeast have mongoloid features" and laughed. That's racist.
A boy said "Bengalis are only good at fish and football". My Bengali friend is good at math.
My grandmother said "don't marry a Punjabi, they are loud and wasteful". Generalisations hurt.
I'm from Goa. A kid asked "do you drink alcohol every day?" No, I'm 10.
My friend from Kerala was called "mallu" in a mocking way. He didn't like it.
A teacher said "Rajasthani art is for tourists, not real art". That's disrespectful.
My mother said "people from Bihar are only good for manual labour". That's a lie.
A kid said "Uttarakhand is just a hill station, no real city". Dehradun is a city.
I'm from Assam. A boy asked "do you have rhinoceros in your backyard?" No, I have a bicycle.
My father said "we don't eat with people from Odisha because they eat dog". That's a disgusting rumour.
A teacher asked a student from Jharkhand "do you speak tribal language?" and made him repeat words.
My friend from West Bengal was told "you look Bangladeshi, go back". He was born in Kolkata.
A boy said "all people from UP are corrupt". My uncle is a policeman and honest.
My grandmother said "South Indians are too dark and short". That's colourism and regionalism.
I'm from Madhya Pradesh. A kid asked "are you from the jungle area?" MP has Bhopal and Indore.
My teacher said "people from Rajasthan are conservative and don't educate girls". My sister is an engineer.
A boy called me "bhangi" because my father cleans streets. My father is a hero.
My friend was told "your family are dhobis (washermen), you'll always smell". That's cruel.
My mother said "don't play with that teli (oil presser) boy". I don't know his caste, only his name.
A kid said "your dad is a nai (barber), so you cut hair". I want to be an astronaut.
My teacher called a student "kumhar" (potter) and said "go make pots". He cried.
My grandmother said "lohars (ironsmiths) are lower caste, don't touch their tools". That's old thinking.
A boy said "chhipi (dyer) people are dirty because of colours". My friend's family runs a business.
My friend is from the sunar (goldsmith) caste. Another kid said "you are greedy like your caste".
I heard my aunt say "we don't marry into mali (gardener) families". Why not? They are kind.
A teacher said "kayastha people are good at writing but not hard work". That's a stereotype.
My mother said "bania people are money-minded and stingy". My bania friend shared his tiffin.
A kid said "you are a gwala (milkman) so you smell of cows". That's not nice.
My grandmother said "khatik (butcher) people are violent". My khatik friend is gentle.
I'm from a farming caste. A boy said "you are a farmer so you must be poor". Many farmers are rich.
My teacher asked a boy from a weaving caste "do you make rugs at home?" He felt ashamed.
A kid said "your father is a cobbler, so you fix shoes". My father fixes shoes and I'm proud.
My mother told me "don't bring that bhadbhunja (grain parcher) boy home". He's my only friend.
My friend was called "chamar" (leather worker) and pushed. He fell and scraped his knee.
A teacher asked a girl from a fishing caste "do you smell like fish?" She stopped coming to school.
My grandmother said "gond (tribal) people are not civilised". That's colonial thinking.
My aunt said "fair skin is like a passport to a good life". What about dark skin?
A kid in my class called me "kala kauwa" (black crow). I don't want to be a crow.
My mother rubs ubtan (turmeric paste) on me every day to "lighten" me. It burns a little.
My father showed a photo of a fair girl and said "see, she is beautiful". I said "I am also beautiful". He didn't reply.
A boy said "dark people are like burnt rotis". That's horrible.
My teacher chose all fair kids for the front row in the school choir. I sing well but I'm in the back.
My grandmother said "if you are fair, you can marry anyone; if dark, you need dowry". That's not true.
I saw a matrimonial site filter for "skin tone: very fair". That's like shopping.
My cousin got fairness treatment at a salon for her wedding. She looked pale and sad.
A kid asked me "why are you so dark? Did you fall in mud?" That's not funny.
My mother compared my baby photos - "you were fair then, what happened?" I grew up.
A relative said "your child is dark but has good features". Backhanded compliment.
My father told my brother "don't play in sun, become dark like your sister". I was there.
I heard my mother say to her friend "her dark skin is my biggest tension for marriage". I'm only 12.
A boy said "fair and lovely, dark and ugly". I want to tear that ad.
My teacher said "you have wheatish complexion, that's acceptable". Acceptable? Not beautiful?
My grandmother gave my fair cousin gold jewellery and me silver. She said "gold suits fair skin".
A kid in my class said "dark people don't need sunscreen". We do, and we burn too.
My aunt said "she is dark but at least her hair is good". There's always a "but".
I want to be an actress but everyone says "only fair girls become heroines". That's changing but slowly.
A kid called me "chink" because I'm from Manipur. I didn't know that word but it hurt.
My mother was told "go back to China" at a Delhi market. She is from Nagaland.
A boy said "all Northeast people eat dogs". My family does not. That's a lie.
My teacher asked me "do you speak Chinese?" I speak Ao and English.
I'm from Mizoram. A kid said "you look like a Korean wannabe". I just look like me.
My father was not given a flat because the landlord said "no foreigners". We are Indian citizens.
A girl in my class said "your eyes are so small, can you see properly?" Yes, I can see your meanness.
My friend from Arunachal was called "mom's cooking" - I didn't get it but he was sad.
A boy pulled his eyes slanted and said "ching chong" to a Sikkimese girl. She cried.
My teacher said "you people from Seven Sisters are very exotic". We are not exhibits.
I'm from Tripura. A kid asked "do you have tribal wars?" No, we have schools.
My mother was told "speak Hindi properly" even though she speaks it well, just with an accent.
A boy said "Northeast girls are easy" - I don't know what that means but it sounded dirty.
My friend from Meghalaya was called "kaalia" even though she is fair-skinned. They just wanted to hurt.
My grandmother said "those people are not real Indians, they look like Burmese". That's wrong.
A teacher asked a boy from Assam "do you have a flat face like a pancake?" The class laughed.
I'm from Nagaland. A kid asked "do you hunt heads?" That's a hundred years ago.
My father was told "you have slanted eyes so you must be good at maths". That's a different stereotype.
A boy said "Northeast people are Christians because they were converted by force". That's not always true.
My mother cried when a man on the metro said "go to your country, chinki". She was born in Dimapur.
My friend is Dalit and the teacher made him sit on the floor while others sat on benches.
A boy said "your father is a scavenger, so you are a scavenger's son". That's his job, not his identity.
My grandmother won't let the Dalit electrician touch anything. She asks him to put tools down and leave.
A kid said "don't drink water after a Dalit, you'll get impure". I drank after him - nothing.
My mother said "I don't want you to play with that boy, his family eats dead animals". That's a rumour.
My uncle said "reservation for SC/ST is destroying our country". My SC friend is top of class.
A teacher called a Dalit boy "ganda" (dirty) for no reason. He hadn't done anything.
My friend's family was not allowed to use the village pond. They had to walk 2km.
A boy said "you are a bhangi so you will always clean toilets". I will be a doctor.
My grandmother said "don't let that child touch the food vessel". I hugged him and then touched the vessel.
In my village, Dalit children have to sit separately at midday meal. I sat with them once and got scolded.
My father said "we are not like them, we are clean". Clean from what? Skin?
A kid said "your caste is for beating drums at weddings". That's a profession, not a person.
My teacher asked a Dalit girl "why are you wearing a bindi? You don't have the right." That's cruel.
I heard my aunt say "these people are born to serve". That's feudal thinking.
A boy said "your name is Mohammed so you must be a terrorist". My name is Mohammed and I like cricket.
My mother told me "don't go near that mosque because they do bad things". What bad things?
My friend stopped eating lunch with me because he said "Muslim food is not halal for me". I don't even eat meat.
A teacher asked a Muslim girl "why don't you take off your hijab? It's a safety hazard". She cried.
I heard my neighbour say "all Muslims should be registered". That's what happened to Jews in Germany.
A kid pulled my skullcap and threw it. I chased him but he laughed.
My grandmother said "Muslims are dirty because they don't bathe after sex". That's not for a child to hear.
My father said "don't wish Eid Mubarak, say Happy Diwali instead". I want to wish both.
A boy in my class said "Quran teaches violence". I've read parts - it teaches kindness.
My mother told me "Muslims are taking over our shops". That shop is owned by a Hindu.
I'm afraid to say my last name is Khan because people will think I'm Pakistani.
A teacher said "Mughals destroyed our temples, so don't be friends with Muslims". That was centuries ago.
My friend was not allowed to join the school football team because "Muslims are aggressive". He's very gentle.
I heard my uncle say "love jihad - be careful of Muslim boys". My Muslim friend just likes to play carrom.
A kid said "all Muslims eat beef and we should kill them". That's so violent. I'm scared.
My grandmother said "don't drink water from a Muslim's house, it's najis". That's not Islamic teaching.
A boy called me "Pakistani" because my family is from Lucknow. That's not how it works.
My teacher said "let's not invite the Muslim boy to the temple visit". He was the only one left out.
My friend's family changed their name from Akhtar to Akash to avoid discrimination.
I saw a social media post saying "Boycott Muslim shops". My uncle's shop is Muslim and he gives free water.
My mother said "don't wear your hijab to the park, someone might snatch it". I'm scared.
A kid said "your Allah is fake, my Ram is real". Why can't both be real?
My father won't let me go to my Muslim friend's house for "safety". His house is safe.
A teacher asked "why do Muslims pray five times? Are you that guilty?" That's disrespectful.
My grandmother said "these people will force us to eat beef one day". No one forces anything.
A boy said "halal meat is cruel". I read that halal is meant to be merciful.
My friend was told "you smell like masjid" because of incense. That's not bad.
I heard my aunt say "Muslims are overbreeding to outnumber Hindus". That's fear-mongering.
My mother hid our Quran when guests came. She said "they will think we are orthodox".
A kid said "your mother wears a black tent". She wears a colourful hijab.
My teacher said "let's not celebrate Eid in school, only Diwali and Holi". That's not secular.
My friend's father was not hired because "Muslims take too many holidays for namaz".
A boy asked me "do you have four wives?" I'm 9. No.
My grandmother said "they cut throats of animals on Bakrid, so cruel". But we also sacrifice goats sometimes.
I'm scared to say "Bismillah" before eating in school because kids might laugh.
A kid said "you are dark and low caste - double bad luck". I felt like crying.
My aunt said "fair Brahmins are the best". That's two prejudices in one.
My teacher said "dark skin and curly hair - you must be from a tribal background". So what?
A boy called me "kaalu chamar" - two slurs together. I reported him.
My grandmother said "only fair-skinned Rajputs are real Rajputs". That's historically wrong.
I'm dark and from a backward caste. My cousin is fair and from same caste. She gets treated better.
My mother said "at least you are not dark and Dalit - that would be worse". That's hierarchy of pain.
A kid said "dark, low caste, and Muslim - you have three strikes". I don't play baseball.
My teacher said "she is dark but from a good family". "Good family" means upper caste.
My friend is fair and Dalit. People say "you don't look Dalit". That's weird.
My mother told me "don't speak Hariyanvi, it's a peasant language". I'm a peasant's daughter and proud.
A boy said "Braj bhasha is for illiterate villagers". It's a literary language.
My teacher corrected my Awadhi words and said "speak standard Hindi". But Awadhi is sweet.
Kids laugh when I say "haan ji" instead of "haan". That's just respect.
My friend from Mewat speaks Mewati. Kids say "you sound like a buffalo herder".
My grandmother said "don't learn Urdu, it's Muslim language". Urdu is for everyone.
A teacher told a student "don't speak Dogri, speak Hindi or fail". That's language oppression.
My father said "English is for success, Hindi is for home, regional is for servants". I'm confused.
A kid said "you speak like a rickshaw puller". My father is a rickshaw puller, and he's smart.
My mother said "don't use Punjabi slang, it's cheap". I like the slang.
My Dalit friend was asked to clean the classroom alone while others played.
A teacher said "you SC/ST students only got in because of seats". That's discouraging.
A boy refused to sit next to a Dalit girl. He said "I might become impure".
My grandmother said "Dalit teachers shouldn't teach our children". Our Dalit teacher is the best.
The school water cooler has a sign "SC/ST students use the tap outside". That's segregation.
My friend was not allowed to participate in a pooja at school because of his caste.
A kid said "your father is a sanitation worker, so you are also a sanitation worker". Not by birth.
My teacher asked a Dalit boy "why do you study? You'll end up sweeping anyway." He dropped out.
I heard my aunt say "Dalits are now demanding everything, like they are kings". They demand equality.
My friend's family was not invited to a wedding because of caste. The bride was his classmate.
My mother said "we will find a fair boy for you, don't worry". I don't want a "fair" boy, I want a kind one.
My aunt showed me a photo of a groom and said "he is fair, so you should say yes". He was 30, I'm 12.
A relative said "dark girls have to bring more dowry". That's illegal and wrong.
My grandmother said "she is dark, so we must find a dark boy for her". Why can't she marry anyone?
I heard my mother say "if you study well, your dark skin won't matter". It matters less anyway.
My friend's sister is fair and got 50 proposals. I'm dark and got none (I'm 10, but still).
A boy in my class said "I will marry only a fair girl". He's dark himself.
My mother applies fairness cream on my face before family photos "for marriage biodata".
My aunt said "she has a good figure but dark skin is a problem". I'm still growing.
My father said "don't worry, in Canada they like dark skin". That's not a solution.
A kid pulled my patka and said "turban head, are you a girl?" I'm a boy.
My teacher said "Sikhs are violent because they carry kirpan". That's a religious article.
A boy called me "sardarji" in a mocking way and said "where is your dhaba?"
My grandmother said "Sikhs are not really part of India, they want Khalistan". That's a few people.
My friend was told "you wear a turban so you must be dumb". He's a maths genius.
A man at the airport made my father remove his turban for security. That's illegal.
A kid said "Sikhs eat only dal makhani and drink lassi". That's a stereotype.
My mother said "don't cut your hair, people will respect you". But they don't respect me with hair.
I was called "towel head" in the playground. I don't wear a towel, it's a dastar.
My uncle was not allowed to ride a bike because of his turban. They said "helmet required". That's bias.
A boy said "Christians are converts, so they are traitors to Hindu culture".
My teacher asked a Christian girl "do you eat beef?" She doesn't, but even if she did, why ask?
My grandmother said "don't go to church, they will brainwash you". I went for a wedding.
A kid said "your Bible is a fake book". That's disrespectful.
My friend was told "you celebrate Christmas because you are westernised". Christmas is Indian too.
My mother said "Christians are only poor people who converted for money". That's not true.
A teacher said "stop singing that Christian hymn in assembly". It was a nice song.
I heard my aunt say "missionaries are destroying our religion". They also run good schools.
A boy asked me "do you worship a dead man on a cross?" Yes, and it's my faith.
My father said "don't bring a Bible inside the house". It's just a book.
A boy said "Bihari means poor and illiterate". My father is an IAS officer from Bihar.
My teacher said "Biharis speak a corrupt form of Hindi". It's a dialect, not corrupt.
Kids call me "Bihari bhaiyya" and ask "how many siblings do you have?" I have one.
My mother was told "Biharis are taking all the government jobs". That's competition, not theft.
A man said "Biharis are responsible for Delhi's pollution". No, it's factories.
My friend from Bihar was called "gawar" (villager). He's more tech-savvy than them.
My grandmother said "Bihari people are short-tempered". My Bihari uncle is calm.
A boy said "Biharis eat only sattu and roti, no vegetables". That's not true.
My teacher asked a Bihari student "do you have electricity in your village?" Yes, and internet.
I'm scared to say I'm from Bihar because people will assume I'm a labourer.
I saw a fairness cream ad where a dark girl becomes happy after becoming fair.
My mother buys "glow and lovely" for me. It says "get fair in 4 weeks". It never works.
A Bollywood movie had a song "gori gori" but no "kali kali" song.
My aunt said "see this actress, she is fair and successful". Many dark actresses are also successful.
The doll I wanted was fair and blonde. The brown doll had torn clothes.
My friend said "only fair people can be air hostesses". That's not the rule anymore.
I saw a matrimonial ad "fair, slim, educated" - in that order.
My grandmother watches TV serials where the good sister is fair, bad sister is dark.
A kid said "you are like a villain in movies - dark". I want to be a hero.
My mother said "if you were fair, I'd put your photo on Facebook". She never posts mine.
My Dalit friend's mother cooked for us. My grandmother threw the food away.
A boy said "I won't eat your lunch because your caste prepares it". I made it myself.
My teacher said "don't share tiffin with lower caste kids". That's so wrong.
My mother said "don't take water from that hand pump, it's used by lower castes".
At a wedding, the lower caste caterers were not allowed to sit and eat. They served and left.
My friend's family was served food on paper plates while others got steel.
A kid said "your dahi (yogurt) is polluted because your mother is from a low caste". Yogurt is yogurt.
My grandmother said "if a Dalit touches the pickle jar, it goes bad". That's superstition.
My teacher gave a lower caste boy leftover food from other plates. That's degrading.
I saw a sign "Shudras eat at the back" at a temple feast. That's still happening.
A boy called me "junglee" because I'm from a tribal community. I live in a city.
My teacher said "Adivasis are primitive". My tribe has its own script and calendar.
A kid said "you speak a funny language" when I spoke Gondi. That's my mother tongue.
My grandmother said "tribal people drink mahua and dance". That's a stereotype.
My father was not given a job because "tribals are not reliable". He has perfect attendance.
A boy asked me "do you sacrifice animals?" Some do, but so do some Hindus.
My mother was told "you look like a forest dweller" as an insult. She is beautiful.
A teacher said "Adivasi children need special education because they are slow". That's racist.
My friend from the Santhal tribe was called "Adiwasi" - the person meant it as a slur.
I'm proud to be Adivasi, but kids make me feel ashamed.
A boy said "your mosque is loud, I hate azaan". I like temple bells.
My teacher said "don't say Inshallah in class, say 'if God wills' in Hindi".
My mother told me "don't go to your friend's house on Friday, they do weird prayers".
A kid said "Muslims don't celebrate any Indian festivals". We celebrate Diwali with lights too.
My grandmother said "these Muslims will cut our cows". We don't have cows.
A man at the park said "don't let your child play with that Muslim boy". I play anyway.
My friend's mother was asked to remove her burkha at the bank. She refused and left.
A teacher said "Muslim names are too long, I'll call you Monty". His name is Abdul Rahman.
My father said "don't say salaam alaikum, say namaste". I say both.
A boy said "your madrassa teaches jihad". My madrassa teaches maths and Arabic.
I heard my aunt say "Muslims are not loyal to India". My uncle is a soldier.
My mother hid my namaz cap before guests came. She said "they will think we are orthodox".
A kid said "your roza makes you weak". I feel strong spiritually.
My grandmother said "those Muslims eat with three fingers - dirty". We wash hands.
A teacher asked "why do you fast? To understand poor?" Yes, that's the point.
My friend was called "mullah" and beaten. He had a black eye.
I'm scared to take my mother's biryani to school. Kids say "it smells like beef". It's chicken.
A boy said "all Muslims have beard and small cap". I'm 10, no beard.
My father's shop was boycotted because of his name. We almost lost everything.
My teacher said "let's skip the chapter on Mughal Empire, it's too Muslim-friendly".
A kid asked me "why do you pray to Mecca? Pray to the sun?" No, we pray to God.
My grandmother said "those people will force their religion on us". No one forces.
I saw a video of a mob shouting "har har Mahadev" while beating a Muslim man. I'm scared of mobs.
My mother told me "don't wear a kurta with a skullcap, wear jeans so you look modern".
A boy said "your religion was spread by sword". That's a lie.
My friend's father was fired because "too many Muslims in the team".
A teacher asked a Muslim girl "do you have to cover your face at home?" No, only outside.
I heard my uncle say "Muslims are our enemy within". That's sad.
My mother said "don't talk about Islam in school, keep it secret". I feel like a spy.
A kid said "your God is moon god". No, we believe in one God.
My Dalit friend is also Muslim. He faces double discrimination.
A boy said "dark, Dalit, Muslim - you are a triple loser". I'm a winner in my heart.
My grandmother said "at least you are not Muslim" to me. But my best friend is Muslim.
A teacher said "lower castes and Muslims are the problem of India". That's hate speech.
My mother told me "don't play with that Bihari Muslim boy". That's three biases.
A kid said "your mother wears hijab, your father is dark, you are from UP - you must be poor". We are middle class.
My uncle said "these people breed like rats". I cried.
My friend was not allowed to join the school assembly because he didn't say "Jai Shri Ram".
A boy said "Christians, Muslims, Dalits - all are same, not real Hindus".
My teacher asked a tribal Christian girl "are you even Indian?" She cried.
I heard my aunt say "don't marry outside caste or religion". Love is love.
My mother said "if you bring home a Muslim friend, don't bring him inside". He waits outside.
A kid said "Bihari people are like cancer". That's a horrible thing to say.
My grandmother said "dark girls should not wear bright colours". I wore red and felt beautiful.
My teacher asked a boy from Jharkhand "do you have a mobile phone?" Yes, iPhone.
A boy called me "slumdog" because I'm from a low-income area.
My mother said "don't learn Urdu script, learn Hindi". Urdu is beautiful.
A kid said "Sikhs are like half-Hindu, half-Muslim". No, they are separate.
My friend from Ladakh was called "Chinese". He is Indian.
My grandmother said "Kashmiris are all traitors". My Kashmiri friend loves India.
A teacher said "Rajasthani people are lazy because of heat". That's a climate stereotype.
A boy asked me "do you speak snake language" because I'm from a snake charmer community.
My mother told me "don't tell anyone we are from a potter caste". I'm proud of our art.
