A kid said "your dad is a rickshaw puller, so you are poor". He earns enough for my school.
My grandmother said "fair people are more intelligent". My dark cousin is a gold medalist.
A teacher asked a Dalit boy "why don't you sit in the back?" He refused.
I saw a news report where a man killed his daughter for marrying a lower caste. That's murder.
My friend from Bihar was told "Biharis are only good for chai and samosa".
A boy said "your mother is a maid, so you are a maid's daughter". I will be a judge.
My mother hides her caste name when filling forms. She says "people are prejudiced".
A kid called me "kaala jaadu" (black magic) because I'm dark. That's nonsense.
My teacher said "reservation is for lazy people". My reserved friend works hardest.
A boy said "tribal people eat monkeys". No, we don't.
My grandmother said "don't marry a person from South India, they are too different".
My father said "don't speak Punjabi in front of guests, speak English".
A kid asked me "why do you have a bindi? Are you married?" No, it's just fashion.
My mother said "fair skin cream is a must for girls". I threw it away.
A teacher asked a Muslim boy "why don't you wear a cap today? Are you ashamed?"
My friend from Nepal was called "Nepali" as a slur. Nepal is a neighbour.
A boy said "dark people are like shadows - they follow". That's creepy.
My grandmother said "don't eat with that caste, you'll get diseases". That's not medical.
A kid said "Biharis have no civic sense". My Bihari cousin recycles everything.
My teacher made fun of a student's village accent. The student stopped talking.
I heard my aunt say "these SC people are getting too arrogant". Arrogant for equal rights?
A boy called me "moti kaal" (fat and black) - two insults.
My mother said "if you were fair, I wouldn't have to worry". Worry about what?
A kid said "your father is a carpenter, so you must be good with hands". I'm good at chess.
My grandmother said "never touch a person from that caste". I touch everyone.
A teacher asked a girl from a fishing caste "do you smell like fish?" She left school.
My friend is from a family of manual scavengers. He is brilliant but nobody plays with him.
A boy said "dark people are like burnt toast". I like toast.
My mother told me "don't wear dark clothes, you will look darker". I wore black anyway.
A kid said "your eyes are small like a pig". That's racist and speciesist.
My grandmother said "only fair girls can be dancers". I dance at home.
A teacher asked a boy from a weaver community "do you make rugs?" He said "I make apps".
I heard my uncle say "these Muslims should go to madrassa only, not normal school".
A boy called me "baloch" (a tribal group) as an insult. I don't even know that group.
My mother said "don't tell people our gotra, they will know our caste".
A kid said "fair people are like milk, dark people are like coal". I cried.
My grandmother said "if you are dark, wear only light colours". I wore neon orange.
A teacher said "tribal children have rhythm but no brains". That's false.
My friend from a barber caste was told "you cut hair, so you have no future". He wants to be an engineer.
A boy asked me "are you from Africa?" I'm from Madhya Pradesh.
My mother said "fairness is God's gift". Then God gave me a different gift.
A kid said "dark people are like night - scary". Night is beautiful.
My grandmother said "don't play with that chamar boy, he will bring bad luck". He brings me toffee.
A teacher asked a Dalit girl "why are you wearing new clothes? Stolen?" That's cruel.
I heard my aunt say "reservation is reverse racism". Helping the oppressed is not racism.
A boy called me "kaleja" (liver) - I don't know what that means but it was mean.
My mother said "study hard so you can get a good groom despite your colour".
A kid said "your father is a cobbler, so you will mend shoes". I will fly planes.
My grandmother said "Muslims are not clean because they don't bathe on certain days". That's a lie.
A teacher asked a Bihari student "do you have a toilet at home?" Yes, and a flush.
My friend from a toddy tapping caste was called "sharab ka thekedar" (liquor contractor). He is 12.
A boy said "dark people are like burnt rotis - only good for the bin". That's disgusting.
My mother told me "don't wear your kurta with namazi cap to school". I wore it anyway.
A kid said "Biharis have no table manners". My Bihari friend uses a fork.
My grandmother said "Sikhs don't cut hair because they are lazy". No, it's a vow.
A teacher asked a boy from a potter community "do you play with clay?" He plays cricket.
I heard my uncle say "these lower caste people are demanding too much". Demand equality.
A boy called me "kaala macchar" (black mosquito). That's silly and mean.
My mother said "fair girls get more presents on Diwali". I got less.
A kid said "your eyes are so small you must be from China". I'm from Arunachal.
My grandmother said "don't marry a South Indian, they are coconut people".
A teacher asked a Muslim girl "do you have to ask your husband to go out?" She's 8.
My friend from a fishing caste was called "machhli" (fish). He hates that.
A boy said "dark skin is like dirt - wash it off". You can't wash off skin.
My mother said "if you were fair, you would have been a model". I can still be a model.
A kid asked me "do you eat with hands like a low caste?" Everyone eats with hands in India.
My grandmother said "tribal people are not civilised". My tribal aunt is a professor.
A teacher said "Bihari students are weak in English". My English is better than hers.
I heard my aunt say "Muslims are breeding to overtake us". That's population panic.
A boy called me "chaiwala" because I'm from UP. My father is an engineer.
My mother said "don't play with that boy, his family is khatik (butcher)".
A kid said "your skin is like a burnt papad". I like papad.
My grandmother said "dark girls should not wear makeup, it looks fake".
A teacher asked a Dalit student "why do you wear a sacred thread? You don't deserve it".
My friend from a weaver caste was told "you can only make clothes, not dreams".
I'm scared to say my full name because it reveals my caste, region, and religion.
My mother said "stop crying about racism, it's always been there". But I want it to change.
A boy said "all Kashmiris are stone pelters and terrorists". My family just wants peace.
My teacher asked me "are you a Pandit or a Muslim?" Why does that matter? I'm just a child.
My grandmother said "don't trust anyone from Kashmir, they might be a separatist". My friend is from Srinagar and loves India.
A kid called me "Kashmiri" as a slur. I am from Jammu, not even Kashmir.
My mother told me "don't say you are from Kashmir in school, people will bully you". I pretend to be from Delhi.
A boy said "Kashmiris are beautiful but dangerous". That's a weird stereotype.
My father was not given a rented house because "Kashmiris might have militant connections". He is a teacher.
I heard my uncle say "Kashmir should be given to Pakistan". That would make my Kashmiri friend stateless.
A teacher asked a Kashmiri Muslim girl "do you support Article 370 removal?" She is 9, she doesn't know politics.
My friend's family moved to Delhi because of militancy. Kids call him "refugee" and laugh.
A boy said "all Kashmiris eat rogan josh and are lazy". Rogan josh is delicious.
My mother told me "don't wear a pheran (Kashmiri cloak) to school, you'll look like a terrorist".
A kid said "Kashmiri Pandits are lucky they left, otherwise they would be dead". That's a horrible thing to say.
My grandmother said "Kashmiri Muslims have betrayed us". Not all of them.
I'm scared to say my family is from Baramulla. People might think we support militancy. We don't.
A boy in my class said "Jats are farmers and stupid". My Jat friend is in the science olympiad.
My mother told me "don't marry a Jat, they are hot-headed". That's a generalisation.
A kid said "non-Jats are weak and can't fight". I don't want to fight, I want to study.
My friend from a Jat family was not allowed to sit with "lower castes" at a wedding.
I heard my uncle say "these Jats have too much land and power". That's jealousy.
A teacher asked a Jat boy "why are you so tall? Jats eat ghee?" That's a stereotype.
My grandmother said "Jat girls are too loud and modern". My Jat friend is quiet.
A boy called me "non-Jat" like it's an insult. I don't even know my caste.
My father said "we don't rent to Jats because they fight over parking". That's not fair.
A kid said "Jats are only good for farming and politics". My Jat friend wants to be a dancer.
A boy called me "Gujjar" and said "go take your buffaloes for a walk". My father is a businessman.
My teacher said "Gujjars are uneducated because they move with animals". My cousin is a doctor.
A kid said "Bakerwals are nomads, they don't belong in cities". We have a house in Jammu.
My mother told me "don't tell anyone we are Gujjar, they will think we are backward".
I heard my aunt say "these Gujjars are encroaching on forest land". That's a land dispute, not our fault.
A boy asked me "do you drink camel milk?" No, I drink cow milk like everyone.
My friend from a Gujjar family was not allowed to join a private school because "they won't adjust". He adjusted well.
A teacher asked a Bakerwal girl "do you go to school in a tent?" No, we have a home.
My grandmother said "Gujjars are short-tempered and violent". My Gujjar uncle is a calm lawyer.
A kid said "nomads are dirty because they live with animals". We bathe every day.
A boy said "Nepalis are watchmen and cleaners". My Nepali friend's father is an engineer.
My teacher asked a Nepali girl "do you have a flat face like a Mongol?" That's racist.
A kid called me "khukri" (Nepali knife) and pretended to stab me. That's violent.
My mother said "don't play with Nepali kids, they eat weird food". They eat dal bhat, same as us.
I heard my uncle say "Nepalis are taking our jobs". They work hard.
A boy asked a Nepali student "do you live in a mountain?" No, he lives in Lajpat Nagar.
My grandmother said "Nepalis are not loyal to India, they love their king". Nepal is a republic now.
A teacher said "Nepali students are good only for sports and physical work". That's stereotyping.
My friend from Nepal was called "chinky" because of his eyes. Nepal is in South Asia.
A kid said "Nepalis are all Gurkhas and brave". That's a positive stereotype, but still a stereotype.
A boy said "Bengalis are fish-eating communists". My Bengali friend doesn't like politics.
My mother told me "don't learn Bengali, it sounds like quacking". That's rude.
A kid called me "Bangladeshi" as a slur. I am from Kolkata, born in Delhi.
My teacher asked a Bengali boy "why is your name so long?" It's a normal name.
I heard my aunt say "Bengalis are too intellectual and arrogant". We are just proud of our culture.
A boy asked an Assamese girl "do you have a rhino in your house?" No, but we have toys.
My grandmother said "Assamese people are Chinese-looking". That's false and hurtful.
A kid said "Bengalis are infiltrators from Bangladesh". My family has been Indian for generations.
My friend from Assam was called "chowmein" because of his eyes. He cried.
A teacher said "Bengali accent is funny". I think all accents are beautiful.
My mother was told by a school "your daughter is dark, she may not fit our fair image". We didn't join.
A teacher gave a fair girl the lead role, even though a dark girl sang better.
My friend was called "blackboard" because of her skin and seating near the blackboard.
A kid said "dark children are from the villages". I was born in Delhi.
My mother said "fair children get more attention from teachers". I noticed it too.
A teacher asked a dark boy "are you from a rural background?" He is from South Delhi.
My grandmother said "don't sit in the sun during recess, you'll become even darker". I want to play.
A boy said "dark girls should not wear pink". I wore pink anyway.
My friend was made to stand at the end of every line because she is dark.
A teacher said "you are dark but your handwriting is good". What does one have to do with the other?
My father was told "we don't rent to people from Uttar Pradesh". That's regional discrimination.
A landlord said "no Muslims allowed in this building". We saw the sign.
My mother cried because a broker said "dark skinned people lower the property value".
A neighbour said "these Biharis make the colony dirty". We clean more than them.
A boy in my colony said "don't play here, this is for upper caste only".
My family was asked to leave a rented house because "too many Dalit visitors came".
A society put a rule "no non-vegetarian cooking". That targeted our Muslim neighbours.
My grandmother said "don't buy a house in that area, it's full of lower castes". I want to live with everyone.
A kid said "your house is in a slum, you are poor". My house is small but clean.
My father was denied a home loan because of his surname. That's illegal.
A policeman asked my brother "why are you out so late? What caste are you?" He was coming from tuition.
My friend was stopped by police for "loitering" while white kids passed by. We are 12.
A police officer said to my father "you look suspicious, show your papers" because we are from Bihar.
My mother was asked "are you a maid?" while walking in a nice neighbourhood. She's a lawyer.
I saw a policeman beat a Dalit man. I hid behind my mother.
My uncle was arrested for "wandering" in a market. He was buying vegetables.
A teacher called the police on a dark-skinned student who lost his bus pass. They treated him like a criminal.
My grandmother said "don't run if police call you, they might shoot". That's scary.
A kid said "my father is a police officer, he can send you to jail because you are from UP".
I'm scared to report bullying because the bully's father is in the police.
A nurse told my mother "dark skin people have high pain tolerance, you don't need medicine". That's a myth.
My grandmother was given a bed near the toilet because she is from a lower caste.
A doctor said to a Muslim woman "remove your hijab, it's a hospital". She felt humiliated.
My friend's brother was not admitted because "Bihari patients default on payments". We have insurance.
A lab technician said "tribal people have different blood groups". No, same as everyone.
My mother was asked "are you sure you can pay?" because of her skin colour.
A nurse called a Dalit patient "ganda" (dirty) and made him wait longer.
My teacher said "hospitals are biased, so always take a fair-skinned family member with you". That's sad.
A boy said "dark people don't get sick because they are strong from working in sun". That's not science.
My grandmother was given less pain medication than a fair patient with the same condition.
A boy said "Biharis have big stomachs because they eat too much sattu". That's body shaming.
My friend from Punjab was called "gabru" but in a mocking way about his height.
A kid said "dark people have big lips like monkeys". That's a racist caricature.
My teacher said "Rajasthani girls are skinny because of drought". That's not funny.
A girl said "your nose is flat like a tribal". That's a racist physical stereotype.
My mother told me "don't grow your hair long, it makes you look like a tribal".
A boy said "dalits have bad teeth because of genetics". No, it's about healthcare access.
My grandmother said "fair people have sharper features". That's a beauty standard, not biology.
A kid said "you have curly hair like a junglee". My curls are beautiful.
My friend was called "chashmish" (spectacled) and "South Indian" - two unrelated things.
My teacher said "with a surname like Prasad, you must be backward caste". I didn't know.
A boy said "Sharma is a Brahmin name, so you think you are superior". I don't.
My mother told me "change your surname to a neutral one for job applications". That's dishonest.
A kid said "Verma means you are a landlord's child, so you are arrogant". I'm not.
My friend with a Dalit surname was not invited to a birthday party.
A teacher said "Singh is a Kshatriya name, so you must be into sports". I like chess.
My grandmother said "don't marry a person with a 'low' surname". I will marry for love.
A boy said "Khan means you are Muslim, so you are a terrorist". That's dangerous.
My mother told me "don't use your full name in online games, people will bully you".
A kid said "Gupta is a baniya name, so you are greedy". My family runs a charity.
My teacher said "speak 'shuddh' Hindi, not this Bhojpuri mix". Bhojpuri is also pure.
A boy laughed at my Haryanvi accent and said "angrezo ke zamane ki bat karo".
My mother told me "don't use Rajasthani words, people will think you are from a village".
A kid said "you say 'hum' instead of 'main', so you are a villager". That's a dialect.
My friend from Lucknow speaks in a polite way. Kids say "you are a nawab wannabe".
A teacher asked a student "why do you roll your R's like a Punjabi?" That's just his accent.
My grandmother said "Pahadi people have a sweet accent but they are slow". That's a stereotype.
A boy said "Bihari people say 'ka kar rahe ho' instead of 'kya kar rahe ho', it's so uneducated".
My mother told me "speak like a Delhi person, not like a UP person". But we are from UP.
A kid said "you say 'hamro' instead of 'mera', are you Nepali?" No, I're from Kumaon.
A boy said "only Jats can be wrestlers, you are too weak". I am good at wrestling.
My teacher said "Sikhs are good at hockey, so you must play hockey". I like badminton.
A kid said "dark people are good at running, not at swimming". That's a lie.
My mother told me "don't join the cricket team, they will make fun of your village background".
A coach said "Muslim boys are good at kabaddi, not at studies". That's wrong.
My friend from a lower caste was not allowed to use the school's sports equipment.
A boy said "Biharis are only good at gully cricket, not proper cricket". My friend plays for state.
My grandmother said "fair girls should not play sports, they will become dark".
A teacher said "tribal children are natural marathon runners". Not all.
A kid said "Punjabis are tall and good at basketball, you are from UP so you are short". I'm average height.
My teacher said "you are from a backward caste, so take vocational training, not science". I want to be a scientist.
A counsellor told a Dalit student "be realistic, take commerce or arts". He is good at maths.
My mother said "lower caste children are pushed into non-academic streams on purpose". I saw that happen.
A boy said "Bihari students go to ITIs, not IITs". My cousin is in IIT.
My teacher said "tribal students should learn handicrafts, not computers". That's limiting.
A kid said "if you are dark, you can't be a pilot. Pilots are fair and tall". That's not true.
My grandmother said "girls from our community should become teachers, not engineers". Why not?
A teacher told a Muslim girl "don't aim for IAS, you will face too much bias". That's discouraging.
My friend from a potter caste was told "you will make pots anyway, so don't waste time in school". He dropped out.
A boy said "Sikhs join army, not law". My uncle is a lawyer.
My aunt said "dark girls need higher dowry to get married". That's a financial penalty for skin colour.
My mother told me "study well so we don't have to pay dowry because you are dark".
A boy in my class said "I will take dowry if the girl is dark". That's illegal.
My grandmother said "fair girls can marry without dowry, dark girls need to compensate". That's disgusting.
A relative said "she is dark, so at least her family is rich - that will work". I'm not a transaction.
My mother cried because a groom's family asked for extra dowry "due to her complexion".
A kid said "dark girls are sold, not married". That's a harsh reality in some places.
My father said "we will save money for your dowry because you are dark". Save for my education instead.
I heard my aunt say "if she were fair, she would have got a doctor groom". I don't care about groom's job.
My grandmother said "in our village, dark girls are married late and with less ceremony". That's unfair.
I have a Bihari mother and a Punjabi father. Kids say "you are a hybrid - which one are you?"
My grandmother said "mixed caste children are confused and weak". I am not confused.
A boy said "half Muslim, half Hindu? That's illegal." It's not, it's love.
My mother is from South India, father from North. Kids say "you are a confusion". I am whole.
A teacher asked me "which caste do you follow?" I follow both.
My friend's parents are from different religions. He is called "two-faced". He is one.
My aunt said "mixed children have identity crisis". I know who I am.
A kid said "you don't belong anywhere". I belong with my family.
My grandmother said "don't marry outside your region, the children will suffer". I suffered from your words.
I'm from a mixed Dalit-OBC family. Relatives say "you are not fully Dalit, so you don't understand". I understand pain.
A boy said "you are low caste and poor - double loser". I am rich in heart.
My teacher said "poor SC students get free mid-day meal, but they don't deserve more". That's cruel.
My mother told me "don't wear cheap clothes, people will assume your caste".
A kid said "your shoes are torn because you are a chamar". No, because my family is saving money.
My friend's family is Dalit and poor. A neighbour said "these people are born to be poor". That's a lie.
My grandmother said "reservation only helps poor, not all lower castes". That's not how it works.
A teacher said "if you are poor and dark, you have two strikes against you". I will strike back with success.
A boy said "your father is a rickshaw puller and you are dark - nobody will hire you". I will hire myself.
My mother said "we are poor but at least not lower caste". That's internalised hierarchy.
A kid said "you look like a beggar" because of my skin and clothes. That's double hate.
A boy called my friend "bhangi" and said "you clean poop, so you are poop". He cried.
My teacher said "Valmiki students should not touch the water cooler". That's untouchability.
My mother told me "don't eat food made by that community, even if it's tasty". I ate it.
A kid said "your father cleans drains, so you smell". He smells of soap after bathing.
My friend's family is from a manual scavenger background. They were denied a bank loan.
My grandmother said "those people are born to do dirty work". No one is born for that.
A teacher said "scavenger caste children should sit in the last row". The principal agreed.
A boy asked me "do you touch human waste with your hands?" No, we have tools.
My mother told me "don't tell anyone our subcaste, just say SC". We are not ashamed.
A kid said "Valmiki people are not allowed in temples". That's a sin.
A bus conductor asked a dark-skinned man "do you have money or will you run away?"
My mother was told "sit at the back" because she is dark and wearing a saree.
A boy said "Biharis take all seats in the metro". That's not true.
My grandmother said "auto drivers charge more if they see dark skin". I saw that happen.
A kid said "Muslims should travel separately to avoid trouble". That's segregation.
My father was asked to show his ticket three times on a train while fair passengers were not checked.
A teacher said "if you are from UP, don't travel alone at night, people will harass you".
My friend was pushed on a bus and called "chamar". He didn't fight back.
A woman moved her bag when my mother sat next to her. She said "I prefer to sit alone".
A boy said "dark people are invisible to taxi drivers". They often skip us.
I wish I was fair so my mother would love me more. Is that true?
My grandmother said "dark children bring bad luck". Am I bad luck?
A boy told me "you are dark because you did something wrong in your past life". Is that real?
My teacher said "if you studied hard, you wouldn't be dark from playing outside". I study hard.
My mother said "you look like a servant's child". I feel like I'm not hers.
A kid said "your skin is like an outcast". Am I an outcast?
My aunt said "nobody will love you because you are dark". Will anyone love me?
My father told me "don't look in the mirror too much, you won't like what you see". That broke me.
A boy said "dark girls are ugly". I think I'm ugly now.
My grandmother said "you are a burden because of your colour". I feel like a burden.
I saw a comment on a video: "Bihari people are cockroaches". That made me cry.
My friend's photo was shared in a group with the caption "look at this kaalu".
A boy said "you are trending on meme pages as a dark joke". I was horrified.
My mother told me "don't post your photos online, people will be racist".
A kid sent me a racist meme about "Bihari chaiwala". I blocked him.
My teacher said "don't report online racism, it's just a joke". It's not a joke.
A boy said "your WhatsApp status is dark like you". I deleted it.
My friend was trolled on Instagram for his "tribal looks". He deactivated his account.
I saw a YouTube video where people ranked states - Bihar was called "most backward".
A girl in my class shared a "fairness filter" video of me without asking.
My teacher said "Sikh students can wear a patka but not bring a kirpan". That's against his faith.
A boy said "Muslims shouldn't be allowed in school during Ramadan because they are tired and slow".
My friend was asked to remove his kara (steel bracelet) because it's "not uniform".
A kid said "all Muslims are like Taliban". That's a group, not a religion.
My mother said "don't wear a kurti with Islamic print, they will bully you".
A teacher asked a Sikh boy "are you a fan of Diljit Dosanjh?" He said "no, I like classical music".
A boy said "Christians go to church to confess sins, so they are all sinners".
My friend was called "Babri masjid destroyer" because he is Hindu. He is 11.
A kid said "Jains are extreme, they don't even kill mosquitoes, so they are useless". That's not kind.
My grandmother said "Buddhists are atheists, they don't believe in anything". That's false.
A local boy said "you are a Dilli wala, you come here and spoil our mountains". We are tourists, not spoilers.
My mother was told "don't buy land here, outsiders are not welcome". That's xenophobia.
A kid said "plains people are loud and dirty". My family is quiet.
My grandmother said "Pahadis think they are superior because of fair skin". That's a reverse stereotype.
A boy said "you are from Punjab, so you will drink and dance here". We don't.
My teacher said "local children are more honest than outsiders". Not always.
A kid called me "pahadi" as a slur when I moved from Shimla to Delhi. I felt torn.
My mother said "don't tell them you are from Himachal, they will think you are a farmer".
A boy said "Uttarakhand is full of labourers who come to Delhi". We are professionals.
My friend from Kullu was called "apple seller" at school. He cried.
My mother said "see, the heroine is fair, that's why she's successful".
A kid said "you are dark like a villain in Dangal". The wrestlers were real.
My grandmother said "Kajol is dark but she is rich, so it's okay". No, she's okay because she's talented.
A boy said "dark actors only play servants in movies". That's changing.
My teacher said "TV serials show fair heroines and dark maids - that's reality". That's a problem.
I saw a movie where a dark girl wears a mask to become fair. That's a terrible message.
A kid said "Bhojpuri films are only for Biharis and they are all dark and loud". That's stereotyping.
My mother said "don't watch movies with dark heroes, they are not aspirational". I like them.
A boy said "Kashmiri heroes are always shown as terrorists in Bollywood". That's not accurate.
My grandmother said "south Indian actors are dark but they dub in Hindi". That's not an insult.
A shopkeeper said "you Biharis don't buy anything, just look". My father bought a TV.
My mother was asked "do you speak Hindi or Bihari?" Bihari is not a language.
A kid said "your food smells like a village". It smells like home.
My teacher said "you have a village mentality" because I asked a simple question.
A boy said "you are from UP, so you must have 10 siblings". I have one.
My grandmother said "dark people are not photogenic". I take good photos.
A neighbour said "these people are not cultured". We have culture for centuries.
A kid asked me "do you have a toilet at home?" Yes, a western one.
My mother was told "you look like a maid" at a parent-teacher meeting.
A boy said "your name is so rustic". My name is Mohan, it's a classic name.
I heard my father say to my mother "she is dark, at least she is obedient".
My aunt said "we should have aborted her, she turned out dark". I overheard. I'm shattered.
My grandmother told my mother "it's your fault, you must have eaten something wrong during pregnancy".
My uncle said "dark girls don't need education, they will do household work". I will study.
My mother said "your dark skin is a punishment from God". What did I do?
My father told my brother "marry a fair girl to improve the family colour". That's eugenics.
My aunt compared me to my fair cousin and said "see the difference". I see.
My grandmother said "you are dark like your father's side, that's where the problem is".
I heard my mother say "I wish she was fair like her friend". I wish she was kind.
My father said "don't wear shorts, your dark legs will show". I'll wear shorts anyway.
My friend's father was beaten for drinking water from an upper caste well.
A boy said "my uncle will kill your family if you sit with us". I was terrified.
My mother told me "don't go to that side of the village, they are upper caste and violent".
I saw a news report of a Dalit man burned alive. I had nightmares.
My grandmother said "lower castes should not complain, they will be killed". That's fear.
A teacher said "caste violence is only in villages, not here". But it happens in cities too.
My friend's house was set on fire because his sister married an upper caste boy.
A kid said "your caste deserves to be beaten". I ran away.
My mother hides my father's caste identity to avoid workplace violence.
I'm scared to go to my village because of caste conflicts.
A teacher said "Bihari students are good at exams because they cheat". That's a stereotype.
My mother said "you have to work harder because you are from UP, people assume you are less smart".
A boy said "Rajasthanis are not good at English". I got 98% in English.
My friend from Haryana was told "you are good only at physical training, not studies". He topped the class.
A teacher said "Punjabi students are not serious about studies, only about fashion". That's not true.
My grandmother said "South Indian students are better at maths because they are dark and hardworking". That's a weird stereotype.
A kid said "you are from Bihar, so you must be good at competitive exams". Pressure.
My mother told me "don't say you are from UP in college, say you are from NCR".
A teacher said "students from Uttarakhand are slow learners". My friend is fast.
My father said "people from Himachal are good at English because of missionaries". Not all.
A kid called me "kala kachua" (black turtle). I don't want to be a turtle.
My friend was called "blacky" by everyone. He stopped coming to play.
A boy said "let's play dark vs fair, dark team are the bad guys". I didn't play.
My mother told me "don't play hide and seek, you are dark and will be hard to find". That's not funny.
A kid said "you are so dark you absorb light". That's a science joke but hurtful.
My grandmother said "dark children are not good at sports because they are slow". My dark friend is fastest.
A boy said "you look like a burnt roti". I threw mud at him.
My friend was called "chaarcoal" - misspelled charcoal. He cried.
A kid said "if you were any darker, you'd be invisible". I felt invisible.
My mother said "don't pay attention to names, they are just jokes". But they hurt.
Is it true that "black is beautiful"? Because everyone around me says the opposite.
My teacher said "all colours are equal". Then why do people behave differently?
I want to be proud of my dark skin, but my mother is not proud. What should I do?
A boy said "dark people are not patriotic". That doesn't make sense.
My grandmother said "this is our culture, not racism". Is calling someone "kaalu" culture?
My friend is from a lower caste and very smart. Why do people not see that?
I'm scared that I will become racist like my parents. How do I stop that?
A teacher said "you are overreacting, it's not racism". When does it become racism?
My mother said "don't talk about caste, it's rude". But ignoring it doesn't help.
I want to tell my grandparents that colour doesn't matter, but they won't listen.
A kid said "you can't change your skin, so just accept being ugly". I don't accept that.
My father said "complaining about racism makes you a victim". But I am a victim.
Is it okay to be angry about racism? My mother says "don't be angry, be successful".
I saw a video of Dr. BR Ambedkar. Why do some people hate him? He fought for us.
My friend said "one day we will have no caste". Is that possible?
My teacher said "India is a melting pot". Then why are we still separate?
A boy said "racism is over, you are just sensitive". Am I sensitive or is he insensitive?
My mother cried when I asked "am I ugly because I'm dark?" She said no, but she cried.
I want to hug every child who is called "kaalu". Can I do that?
My grandmother said "things will change when you grow up". But I want change now.
A boy said "dark, Muslim, and from Kashmir - you are three times a target".
My mother told me "don't be friends with a Dalit Christian, they are at the bottom of everything". That's hierarchy.
A kid said "your father is a Dalit and your mother is tribal - your family is double low". I love them.
My teacher asked "how can you be a Hindu and from a scavenger caste?" That's common.
A boy said "you are a Muslim from UP - you must be a cow smuggler". No.
My grandmother said "Sikh Dalits are not real Sikhs". They are.
A kid said "OBC Muslims are fake Muslims". No community is fake.
My mother said "we are Rajput, so we are superior to all". That's arrogance.
A boy called me "chamar sikh" - two slurs. I reported.
My friend is a Dalit Christian from a tribal background. He says "I belong nowhere". I said "you belong with me".
A teacher said "Adivasi Christians are converted, so they lost their culture". They kept their culture.
A kid said "Kashmiri Pandit, so you are a refugee". We are citizens.
My grandmother said "Bihari Muslims are more conservative". That's a generalisation.
A boy said "you are a dark-skinned Brahmin - that's an oxymoron". No, it's not.
My mother said "we are Thakur, but at least not Rajput". Internal hierarchy.
